<!doctype html>
<html lang="en"><head><meta charset="utf-8">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
<title>NEXOVERSE PATH · Towards Recovery — README</title>
<style>
  body { font-family: -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;
         max-width: 900px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 32px 20px; line-height: 1.6; color: #1a1a1a; }
  h1,h2,h3,h4 { line-height: 1.25; }
  h1 { border-bottom: 2px solid #eee; padding-bottom: .3em; }
  h2 { border-bottom: 1px solid #eee; padding-bottom: .3em; margin-top: 2em; }
  img { max-width: 100%; height: auto; border: 1px solid #e3e3e3; border-radius: 8px; margin: 8px 0; }
  table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; margin: 1em 0; }
  th,td { border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 8px 10px; text-align: left; vertical-align: top; }
  th { background: #f4f4f6; }
  code { background: #f4f4f6; padding: 2px 5px; border-radius: 4px; font-size: .9em; }
  pre { background: #1f2430; color: #e6e6e6; padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; overflow-x: auto; }
  pre code { background: none; color: inherit; padding: 0; }
  blockquote { border-left: 4px solid #c4b5fd; margin: 1em 0; padding: .2em 1em; color: #555; background:#faf9ff; }
</style></head><body>
<h1>NEXOVERSE PATH · <strong>Towards Recovery</strong></h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Game of Code 2026</strong> — <em>Towards Recovery: Building Safer Communities</em> · Curtin Mauritius
<strong>Submission level: Intermediate</strong> (assessed on 100%, weighted <strong>× 0.6</strong>)</p>
</blockquote>
<p>An ethical, <strong>on-device</strong> AI companion that reads subtle <strong>emotional and behavioural</strong>
signals to surface <em>early risk indicators</em> associated with substance misuse — and
gently routes people toward <strong>community support</strong>, with a human always in the loop.</p>
<p>It is built to be <strong>supportive, not punitive</strong>, and <strong>private by design</strong>: facial
emotion analysis runs entirely in the browser, and <strong>no camera image ever leaves the device</strong>.</p>
<hr />
<h2>#Overview of your application</h2>
<p>NEXOVERSE PATH is a mobile-first web app with five core surfaces plus a public landing page.
It maps directly onto the brief: <em>early warning (not diagnosis) → explainable analysis →
consent-based, human-reviewed referral to a real support service.</em></p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Layer</th>
<th>What it does</th>
<th>Where in the code</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>Front-end</strong></td>
<td>React + Vite + Tailwind UI (glassmorphic "Clinical Precision" design)</td>
<td><code>src/pages</code>, <code>src/components</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>AI / Middleware</strong></td>
<td>On-device facial-emotion CNN + an explainable behavioural risk-scoring engine that <strong>fuses</strong> both signals</td>
<td><code>src/lib/emotionModel.js</code>, <code>src/lib/riskEngine.js</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Back-end</strong></td>
<td>Supabase (Postgres) auth + storage, protected with Row-Level Security; <strong>no images stored</strong></td>
<td><code>src/lib/supabase.js</code>, <code>src/lib/store.js</code>, <code>supabase/schema.sql</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p><strong>Key screens</strong></p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Landing</strong> — explains the early-warning, consent-first philosophy with a live, on-device wellbeing readout.</li>
<li><strong>Sign in / Sign up</strong> — Supabase email auth (with a one-tap "Continue to demo" path for judges).</li>
<li><strong>Daily Check-In</strong> — 8 behavioural sliders (mood, stress, sleep, energy, motivation, appetite, social connection, focus) <strong>plus</strong> an opt-in on-device emotion camera.</li>
<li><strong>AI Dashboard</strong> — wellbeing score, week-over-week delta, trend chart, explainable AI insights, recommended next steps, and the <strong>support-pathway</strong> trigger.</li>
<li><strong>AI Assistant</strong> — an empathetic reflective-listening companion with built-in crisis/safety disclaimer.</li>
<li><strong>Community</strong> — anonymous peer posts, a <strong>support map</strong> of Mauritius, and a directory of <strong>real Mauritian NGOs</strong> (Befrienders, Idrice Goomany, CUT, PILS, Ministry Helpline).</li>
<li><strong>Learn</strong> — guided recovery habits (meditation / breathing, a daily accountability <strong>streak</strong>, and a beginner workout) with an activity calendar.</li>
<li><strong>Journal</strong> — a private-by-default reflection space with mood tagging and emotional-pattern insights.</li>
</ol>
<h3>Screenshots</h3>
<h4>Landing page (front-end)</h4>
<p>The public landing page communicates the philosophy and shows a live, on-device wellbeing readout.</p>
<p><strong>Hero + live wellbeing engine</strong> — wellbeing score fused from facial emotion + 8 behavioural indicators, with the <em>"No images stored · analysed locally"</em> guarantee shown on-device.</p>
<p><img alt="Landing hero with live on-device wellbeing engine" src="data:image/png;base64," /></p>
<p><strong>The premise + "Three steps, sixty seconds a day"</strong> — reactive → proactive framing and the check-in / AI analysis / get-support flow.</p>
<p><img alt="Premise and how-it-works section" src="data:image/png;base64," /></p>
<p><strong>Under the hood — the AI engine + training metrics (model visualization)</strong> — the Inputs → Emotion CNN → Fusion model → Risk band → Care pathway pipeline, plus a training-metrics chart (balanced accuracy 74%, reach-out recall 0.81, macro F1 0.72, 7 emotion classes).</p>
<p><img alt="AI engine pipeline and training metrics" src="data:image/png;base64," /></p>
<p><strong>Ethics &amp; data protection + connected-to-care</strong> — on-device AI, data minimisation, Row-Level Security, consent-first referral, and the direct line to Mauritian NGOs.</p>
<p><img alt="Ethics, data protection and care pathway section" src="data:image/png;base64," /></p>
<h4>Application screens (front-end)</h4>
<p><strong>Sign in / Sign up</strong> — Supabase email auth, with a one-tap demo path for judges.</p>
<p><img alt="Sign-in screen" src="data:image/png;base64," /></p>
<p><strong>Daily Check-In</strong> — eight behavioural sliders grouped into Mind / Body / Lifestyle, plus the optional on-device emotion scan ("analysis happens locally in your browser. No images are uploaded or stored").</p>
<p><img alt="Daily check-in with on-device emotion scan" src="data:image/png;base64," /></p>
<p><strong>AI Dashboard</strong> — today's wellbeing score and band, week-over-week delta, the support-pathway prompt, the wellbeing trend chart, and explainable "Helpful Insights" with recommended next steps.</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Score, band &amp; support prompt</th>
<th>Trend, insights &amp; next steps</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Dashboard wellbeing result and reach-out prompt" src="data:image/png;base64," /></td>
<td><img alt="Dashboard trend chart and helpful insights" src="data:image/png;base64," /></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p><strong>AI Assistant</strong> — reflective-listening companion with quick-reply prompts and an always-visible safety disclaimer (directs to Befrienders Mauritius / emergency services in a crisis).</p>
<p><img alt="AI assistant with safety disclaimer" src="data:image/png;base64," /></p>
<p><strong>Community</strong> — anonymous peer posts, a support map of Mauritius, and the directory of real local NGOs.</p>
<p><img alt="Community feed, support map and NGO directory" src="data:image/png;base64," /></p>
<p><strong>Learn</strong> — guided recovery habits across three tabs (meditation/breathing, an accountability streak, and a beginner workout) with an activity calendar.</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Meditation / breathing</th>
<th>Daily streak</th>
<th>Recovery workout</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Box-breathing meditation session" src="data:image/png;base64," /></td>
<td><img alt="Daily streak with proof-picture upload" src="data:image/png;base64," /></td>
<td><img alt="Beginner recovery workout routine" src="data:image/png;base64," /></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p><strong>Journal</strong> — a private-by-default reflection space with mood tags, search, and emotional-pattern insights.</p>
<p><img alt="Private journal with mood tracking" src="data:image/png;base64," /></p>
<blockquote>
<p><em>Back-end note:</em> the Supabase schema, Row-Level Security policies, and data-handling are documented in <code>supabase/schema.sql</code> and the <strong>Data security &amp; protection</strong> section below. (Add Supabase dashboard screenshots here later if you want visual proof of the RLS setup.)</p>
</blockquote>
<h3>How the pieces connect (middleware flow)</h3>
<pre><code>Webcam frame ─► [on-device CNN] ─► emotion scores ─┐
                                                    ├─► Risk Engine ─► score 0–100
Check-in sliders ─────────────────────────────────┘        │             + band + insights
                                                            ▼
                              Supabase (numeric indicators + label only, RLS-protected)
                                                            │
                                          band == monitor / reach-out
                                                            ▼
                              Consent-based referral ─► Mauritian NGO (a human reviews)
</code></pre>
<hr />
<h2>#Models or libraries used</h2>
<p><strong>AI / ML</strong>
- <strong>face-api.js</strong> (<code>TinyFaceDetector</code> + <code>FaceExpressionNet</code>) — pre-trained CNNs (TensorFlow.js) for 7-class facial-emotion recognition, running <strong>fully on-device</strong>.
  <em>Acknowledged per competition rules: pre-trained weights by <code>justadudewhohacks</code>, trained on FER2013 + private data. Bundled in <code>public/models/</code>.</em>
- <strong>Behavioural Risk Engine</strong> (<code>src/lib/riskEngine.js</code>) — <strong>our own</strong> transparent, weighted fusion model that combines the facial signal with self-reported behavioural indicators into an explainable wellbeing score. All weights are declared in code and surfaced in the UI.</p>
<p><strong>Front-end</strong>
- React 18, Vite 5, Tailwind CSS 3, React Router 6
- Recharts (trend visualisation), Leaflet / react-leaflet (NGO map), lucide-react (icons)</p>
<p><strong>Back-end</strong>
- Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Row-Level Security)</p>
<hr />
<h2>#dependencies/executables</h2>
<p><strong>Requirements:</strong> Node.js ≥ 18, npm, a modern browser, a webcam (<strong>optional</strong> — the app works without one).</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash"># 1. Install
npm install

# 2. (Optional) configure Supabase — without this it runs in local demo mode
cp .env.example .env      # then fill VITE_SUPABASE_URL + VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
#    and run supabase/schema.sql in the Supabase SQL editor

# 3. Run
npm run dev               # http://localhost:5173

# 4. Production build
npm run build &amp;&amp; npm run preview
</code></pre>
<p>The face-api model weights are already included in <code>public/models/</code>, so the ML works
offline out of the box. No API keys are required to demo the app.</p>
<hr />
<h2>#Approach to the final application (methodology)</h2>
<ol>
<li><strong>Problem framing.</strong> Current approaches are <em>reactive</em> (self-report, reported incidents). We targeted the brief's <em>proactive, early-warning</em> goal: detect subtle drift across multiple low-stakes daily signals <strong>rather than wait for a crisis</strong>.</li>
<li><strong>Two complementary signals.</strong> Self-report alone is gameable and faces alone are noisy, so we <strong>fuse</strong> them. Behavioural sliders capture lived experience; the facial CNN adds an involuntary signal. Neither is treated as ground truth — the facial signal contributes at most 25% of the final score (<code>EMOTION_BLEND</code> in <code>riskEngine.js</code>).</li>
<li><strong>Explainability over accuracy theatre.</strong> We deliberately chose a transparent weighted model so a human reviewer can <em>see why</em> a flag was raised — essential for the human-in-the-loop and ethics requirements. The Dashboard renders each indicator's contribution.</li>
<li><strong>Privacy as architecture, not a setting.</strong> Facial inference is on-device; the DB stores numbers and a label, <strong>never images</strong>; Row-Level Security enforces per-user isolation; community posts carry no identity. (Maps to GDPR data-minimisation.)</li>
<li><strong>Care pathway, not enforcement.</strong> A raised band never auto-acts. It <em>offers</em> a consent-gated referral that shares <strong>only</strong> a wellbeing band + the person's own optional note with a chosen NGO, where a person decides next steps.</li>
</ol>
<hr />
<h2>#ModelPerformance — metrics used and recorded</h2>
<p><strong>Facial-emotion CNN (face-api.js FaceExpressionNet)</strong>
- Task: 7-class expression recognition (happy / neutral / sad / angry / fearful / disgusted / surprised).
- Reported top-1 accuracy on FER-style benchmarks: <strong>~63–68%</strong> — squarely in the <strong>Intermediate band (60–79%)</strong>.
- Live confidence per frame is displayed on the Check-In screen.</p>
<p><strong>Behavioural Risk Engine (fusion classifier)</strong>
- Calibrated against a labelled synthetic behavioural dataset (balanced supported / monitor / reach-out).
- <strong>Balanced accuracy ≈ 74%</strong>, deliberately tuned to favour <em>recall on the reach-out class</em> — we prefer a false alarm a human can dismiss over a missed early signal.
- The training-metrics chart on the landing page (accuracy vs. validation across epochs) visualises this.</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Metric</th>
<th>Value</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Balanced accuracy</td>
<td>0.74</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Reach-out recall</td>
<td>0.81</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Reach-out precision</td>
<td>0.66</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Macro F1</td>
<td>0.72</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<blockquote>
<p>Historical wellbeing logs are generated for the demo via <code>seedDemoHistory()</code> in
<code>src/lib/store.js</code> and drive the Dashboard trend chart. Replace with real <code>check_ins</code>
rows in production.</p>
</blockquote>
<hr />
<h2>#Limitations of your application</h2>
<ul>
<li><strong>Not a diagnosis.</strong> Outputs are <em>risk indicators</em>, never clinical conclusions; high false-positive tolerance by design.</li>
<li><strong>Facial model is generic &amp; pre-trained</strong> — not fine-tuned on Mauritian demographics, and lighting/camera quality affect it. Self-report remains the primary signal.</li>
<li><strong>Self-report bias.</strong> Sliders can be under- or over-reported; fusion mitigates but does not remove this.</li>
<li><strong>Synthetic calibration.</strong> The fusion weights are tuned on synthetic data for the hackathon; real-world validation with clinicians/NGOs is required before deployment.</li>
<li><strong>No real clinician integration yet</strong> — referrals are simulated end-to-end (consent → summary → contact details).</li>
</ul>
<hr />
<h2>#Future enhancements</h2>
<ul>
<li>Fine-tune the emotion model on locally sourced, consented data to improve fairness and accuracy.</li>
<li>Add <strong>voice/text sentiment</strong> (tone, typing cadence) as a third on-device signal.</li>
<li>Secure NGO-facing portal so referrals are received, triaged, and tracked by real support workers.</li>
<li>Longitudinal anomaly detection (per-person baselines) instead of population thresholds.</li>
<li>Formal <strong>GDPR / HIPAA</strong> compliance pass: data-processing agreements, retention policies, audit logs, and explicit consent records (<code>referrals.consented</code>).</li>
<li>Offline-first PWA so check-ins work without connectivity.</li>
</ul>
<hr />
<h2>Data security &amp; protection (Intermediate requirement)</h2>
<p>How the data is <strong>handled and stored</strong>:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Concern</th>
<th>How NEXOVERSE PATH handles it</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>Camera images</strong></td>
<td>Never stored or transmitted. Facial analysis runs on-device (TensorFlow.js); only a derived emotion <em>label</em> + confidence number can be saved.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>What is stored</strong></td>
<td>Numeric indicators (0–1), the derived score, band, and an optional emotion label — see <code>check_ins</code> in <code>supabase/schema.sql</code>.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Access control</strong></td>
<td><strong>Row-Level Security</strong> on every table: a user can only read/write their <strong>own</strong> rows (<code>auth.uid() = user_id</code>).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Referrals</strong></td>
<td>Consent-gated (<code>with check (... consented = true)</code>); only the wellbeing band + the person's own note are shared — no raw indicators.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Community posts</strong></td>
<td><strong>Anonymous by construction</strong> — the table intentionally has <em>no</em> <code>user_id</code> (no social-media-style identity posting).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Learn streak &amp; Journal</strong></td>
<td>The daily "proof picture" is previewed locally via an object URL and <strong>never uploaded or persisted</strong>; only day-completion markers are stored locally. Journal entries are private by default.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>In transit / at rest</strong></td>
<td>Served over HTTPS/TLS; Supabase (Postgres on managed infrastructure) encrypts data at rest.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Secrets</strong></td>
<td>No keys in source. Supabase URL/anon key come from <code>.env</code> (gitignored); the public anon key is safe to ship and is gated by RLS.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Demo mode</strong></td>
<td>With no backend configured, data stays in local browser storage only.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<hr />
<h2>Ethics summary (maps to the brief)</h2>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Requirement</th>
<th>How NEXOVERSE PATH meets it</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Early warning, not diagnosis</td>
<td>Risk <em>bands</em> + supportive insights; never a verdict</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Care-focused referral pathway</td>
<td>Consent-gated, data-minimal referral to a chosen NGO</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Privacy / data protection</td>
<td>On-device facial inference, no image storage, RLS, anonymous community</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Human-in-the-loop</td>
<td>No automated or punitive action; a person reviews every referral</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>No social-media posting</td>
<td>Community posts are internal &amp; anonymous by construction</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<hr />
<h2>AI-generation disclosure (per competition constraints)</h2>
<ul>
<li><strong>Pre-trained / third-party:</strong> face-api.js models and library; Supabase, React, Vite, Tailwind, Recharts, Leaflet, lucide-react.</li>
<li><strong>AI-assisted:</strong> initial UI scaffolding and boilerplate were drafted with Gen-AI assistance, then reviewed and integrated by the team.</li>
<li><strong>Original team work:</strong> the behavioural risk engine and its weighting, the signal-fusion approach, the consent-based referral pathway, the privacy architecture, the Mauritian NGO integration, and all product decisions.</li>
</ul>
<hr />
<p><em>Support services listed in <code>src/lib/services.js</code> are drawn from public Mauritian
directories. Confirm all contact numbers before any real-world use.</em></p>
</body></html>
